mapping.a_element: multiplicative (log-scale) reversion process generating a lognormal concentration backbone
mapping.b_element: log price as the dynamical variable with Geometric Brownian Motion generating lognormal price densities

mapping.a_element: implied diffusion evolution of the PM2.5 density
mapping.b_element: Fokker-Planck equation (no-jump limit of the Kolmogorov-Feller equation) for the log-price density

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is a generic multiplicative/log-scale diffusion substrate yielding lognormal distributions, which is not the distinctive machinery of either paper. Paper A's core mechanism is critical slowing down of a finite-memory mean-reverting process detected via early-warning resilience signals (rising autocorrelation, variance, weakened restoring force), whereas Paper B's core mechanism is entropic-inference derivation of a Merton jump-diffusion plus option pricing (Esscher transform, no-arbitrage martingale selection); A has no jump channel, entropic inference, or pricing, and B has no restoring force, criticality, or resilience, so no role-for-role correspondence holds on the driving machinery.
